subject: Geography
title: The Mercator Projection
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Mercator Projection usable.

hook.text: In 1569, Gerardus Mercator solved a problem: how to draw straight sailing routes on flat paper.

buildup.text: His projection stretches the poles so that compass bearings appear as straight lines.

discovery.text: Sailors could draw a line on a Mercator map and follow the compass heading directly.

twist.text: The tradeoff: Greenland looks the size of Africa. In reality, Africa is 14 times larger.

climax.text: Mercator's map became the default world view, distorting how billions perceive geography.

punchline.text: A map for sailors warped the world for everyone.

quiz.question: What distortion does the Mercator projection create?
options: It makes areas near the poles appear much larger
